factors.name: CPI Surprise & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Headline CPI accelerated to 4.2% YoY (from 3.8% prior), exceeding expectations per FXStreet/Bloomberg. This is the fastest pace in three years, driving hawkish Fed repricing and likely higher-for-longer rates—headwind for rate-sensitive Dow names (GE, MMM, HON, financials).

factors.name: Iran Escalation & Strait of Hormuz Closure
factors.summary: US launched second consecutive day of strikes on Iran; Tehran formally closed Strait of Hormuz to all vessels—disrupting ~20% of global oil flows. Bloomberg reports oil surged and Asian stocks fell; geopolitical risk premium elevated. Energy input costs and supply-chain uncertainty weigh on industrials and consumer discretionary.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike
factors.summary: Brent and WTI jumped 2%+ on Hormuz closure threat and strike escalation. Elevated energy costs pressure transportation, manufacturing margins—directly negative for CAT, BA, MMM, and broad cyclical exposure in the Dow's industrial-heavy portfolio.

factors.name: Tech Sector Selloff & Earnings Pressure
factors.summary: Oracle stock declined post-earnings despite EPS beat; revealed $40B planned debt/equity raise for AI capex, raising balance sheet leverage concerns. Micron/Intel weakness escalated pullback to 'correction' phase per MarketWatch. MSFT (Dow component) exposed to tech valuation rerating and capex scrutiny.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Rising (Nominal)
factors.summary: Hot CPI and hawkish Fed expectations push nominal yields higher. 10-year yield firmer; this erodes valuations on dividend-heavy Dow names (KO, PG, JNJ) and financials (GS, JPM), which have underperformed in rising-rate environments.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: DXY trades firmly above 100.00 post-CPI per FXStreet. Strong dollar pressures Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure; translation headwinds for multinational earnings (BA, CAT, MMM, GE, MSFT).

factors.name: VIX & Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Geopolitical escalation and tech correction triggering safe-haven flows. Oil volatility, bond repricing, and equities weakness in Asia signal risk-off positioning—typically drives rotation out of cyclicals and toward safe havens, not supporting Dow.

factors.name: Price Action—4h/24h Decline Underway
factors.summary: US30 down 0.58% in 4h and 1.42% in 24h. Move is already in progress; this is Phase 2, not fresh entry. Market has already partially priced the CPI/Iran risks.

factors.name: Corporate Capital Raising (Oracle, others)
factors.summary: Oracle's $40B planned raise signals high debt/equity issuance in pipeline. Multiple Form 144 insider sales across tech/semis noted. Capital dilution and insider selling add subtle downside pressure.

factors.name: Forward Guidance Uncertainty
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings or forward guidance released in past 24h (Oracle was exception, but guidance already priced as cautious). Lack of clarity on cyclical health (CAT, BA, DE, HON) leaves room for disappointment.

key_levels.resistance: 50200, 50500 (prior day highs, Bollinger upper band zone)

screening.narrative: US30 is caught in a near-term bearish squeeze: hot inflation (CPI 4.2% YoY) has reignited Fed hawkish bets and pushed nominal yields higher, pressuring Dow's dividend-heavy and rate-sensitive names. Simultaneously, US–Iran escalation and the formal Strait of Hormuz closure threaten stagflationary shock—spiking oil and input costs while dampening growth. Tech earnings disappointment (Oracle, Micron, Intel) and the tech correction phase amplify equity risk-off. The index is down 1.42% in 24h (Phase 2 underway), with price action already reflecting the initial shock. DXY strength above 100.00 adds multinational translation headwind for ~50% of Dow revenue exposure.
catalysts_ahead: Fed pivot signals / Kevin Warsh appointment commentary (rate expectations hinge on this)
Iran ceasefire or escalation update (Trump signaled talks ongoing; any breakthrough/breakdown pivotal)
ISM Manufacturing/Services PMI release (next week; critical for cyclical outlook)
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production (input into Dow cyclical health)
Further Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, BA, CAT guidance critical for forward view)
Treasury auction data / 10-year yield momentum (key for dividend and financials valuations)
Oil price stabilization or spike (Hormuz closure resilience test)
Strait of Hormuz status update (shipping/tanker flows resumption would ease geopolitical premium)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran/US announce de-escalation pact and Hormuz reopens → oil crashes, geopolitical premium collapses, equities rally; (2) Fed signals imminent cut or Warsh dovish surprise → yields tank, rate-sensitive Dow names rally; (3) CPI-driven spike in commodities/energy proves transitory → inflation fears fade and cyclicals recover; (4) Earnings from CAT, BA, JPM, GS beat with strong guidance → indices hold support and reverse higher. Watch for any Trump statement on 'deal imminent' with Iran (historically creates whipsaw rallies).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2—already down 1.42% in 24h. Rather than chase shorts, wait for a retest of resistance at 50000–50200 (prior day highs) or a dead-cat bounce on any Iran ceasefire headlines; short that level with conviction on follow-through break below 49500. If Hormuz reopens or Fed surprises dovish, cover and flip to long bias at 49000 support.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk - Iran Conflict Escalation
factors.summary: US launching second consecutive day of strikes on Iran, with Iran ordering complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels. Bloomberg reports global junk debt warning on stagflation risk. This directly threatens ~20% of global oil flows and multinational Dow components with heavy international exposure (~50% of Dow revenues).

factors.name: Oil Price Shock & Input Costs
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports oil surging after US strikes on Iran and Strait of Hormuz closure. Higher energy prices squeeze margins for industrials (CAT, MMM, HON) and increase transportation costs across Dow's real-economy exposure. Strait closure impacts CVX operations and raises stagflation fears.

factors.name: Inflation Data - Hot CPI
factors.summary: FXStreet reports hot US inflation fueling higher-for-longer Fed rate expectations, with headline CPI at 4.2% YoY (vs 3.8% prior) and 0.5% MoM. This pressures rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend-paying utilities, while raising input costs for industrials.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Multiple sources note hot inflation fueling expectations for higher-for-longer Fed rates. Japanese Yen hitting 6-week low near 160.50 on Fed hawkish bets. This tightens financial conditions for Dow's rate-sensitive components and increases discount rates for equity valuations.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Rising
factors.summary: Hot inflation driving yields higher (implied by Yen weakness and Fed rate expectations), pressuring Dow's financials, dividend aristocrats, and raising borrowing costs for capital-intensive industrials like Boeing and Caterpillar.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 160.55, FXStreet notes USD strength after inflation report. Stronger dollar translates to FX headwinds for Dow's ~50% international revenue base, heavier multinational exposure than SPY. Directly impacts CAT, BA, JNJ, AAPL earnings translation.

factors.name: Tech Sector Weakness
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Micron and Intel dragging tech sector into new bearish phase, now officially a correction. Oracle stock slides despite earnings beat on AI spending concerns. Affects Dow tech components (AAPL, MSFT, CRM, CSCO).

factors.name: Stagflation Risk
factors.summary: Bloomberg explicitly flags 'global junk debt flashes warning on growing risk of stagflation' from Middle East conflict. Worst case scenario for Dow's cyclical industrial composition - combines growth slowdown with persistent inflation squeezing margins.

factors.name: Supply Chain Disruption Risk
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz closure threatens freight costs and supply chains. Trans Mountain pipeline hitting full capacity signals global crude flow disruptions. Increases costs for Dow's manufacturing and transportation-heavy components.

factors.name: Safe Haven Flows Away from Equities
factors.summary: Gold falling to multi-month low below $4,050 despite geopolitical risk suggests investors favoring cash/short-term treasuries over all risk assets. Bloomberg notes oil gains but risk assets under pressure - classic risk-off into defensive positioning.

factors.name: China Demand Concerns
factors.summary: Middle East conflict and oil shock threatens China growth trajectory, critical for Dow industrials like CAT, BA, materials demand. No positive China PMI or stimulus data in news flow to offset geopolitical drag.

factors.name: Amazon Logistics Expansion
factors.summary: CNBC reports Amazon trucking expansion sparks freight stock sell-off, posing growing threat to industry incumbents. Competitive pressure on Dow's real-economy logistics exposure and industrials reliant on freight economics.

factors.name: Recent Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: US30 down -0.58% in 4h and -1.42% in 24h, already in downward trajectory. Move underway but not fully extended yet, suggesting room for further downside as geopolitical and inflation narratives compound.

confirmation.narrative: Dow faces a toxic combination of geopolitical escalation (Iran strikes, Strait of Hormuz closure threatening oil supplies), hot inflation data (CPI 4.2% YoY) cementing higher-for-longer Fed policy, and rising stagflation risk. The index's heavy industrial and multinational composition (~50% international revenues) makes it particularly vulnerable to oil shocks, dollar strength (USD/JPY 160.50), and supply chain disruptions. Bloomberg's explicit stagflation warning on global junk debt reflects growing concern that energy price shocks will combine with slowing growth - the worst environment for Dow's cyclical components. Price action confirms the bearish setup with -1.42% decline in 24h, though the move is still in Phase 2 rather than fully extended.
catalysts_ahead: Further Iran conflict developments and Strait of Hormuz status (next 24-48h)
Oil price trajectory and potential SPR release announcements
Fed speakers' commentary on inflation data and policy path
China economic data releases (PMI, industrial production)
Dow component earnings guidance updates
Any ceasefire negotiations or escalation in Middle East
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) credible Iran ceasefire announced reopening Strait of Hormuz, collapsing oil prices; (2) Fed speakers push back on hawkish rate expectations despite hot CPI; (3) US30 reclaims 50,500 with conviction on risk-on rotation; (4) dollar reverses sharply lower reducing FX headwinds. Trump's repeated claims that 'deal is close' creates headline risk for sudden sentiment reversal.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-1.42% in 24h); wait for any relief rally toward 50,200-50,500 resistance zone before adding short exposure, or enter on breakdown below 49,500 support with tight stops.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Second day of US strikes on Iran, Iran closing Strait of Hormuz, widening attacks on Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan. Multiple sources confirm active military conflict undermining risk appetite and creating supply-chain uncertainty for Dow's industrial/cyclical heavyweights (CAT, BA, CVX exposure).

factors.name: Oil price spike
factors.summary: WTI rising to ~$91/bbl (per FXStreet 00:42 GMT) driven by Middle East conflict. Oil input costs rising for transportation, chemicals, energy-intensive industrials; negative for margins. CNN notes 'investors brace for long grind' and energy disruption risks.

factors.name: Inflation surprise & hawkish ECB
factors.summary: Trump statement 'loves the inflation' as US prices rise at fastest rate in 3 years (per BBC). ECB set to hike for first time since 2023 due to Iran-war-driven inflation (Bloomberg 04:15). Higher real yields globally pressures valuation-heavy equities; Dow financials (JPM, GS) face margin compression.

factors.name: Copper & industrial metals decline
factors.summary: Copper fell to 3-week low (Bloomberg 03:41) on flare-up fears and expected slower growth. Signals weakening industrial demand and recession concerns—bearish for CAT, MMM, HON guidance.

factors.name: Dollar weakness despite safe-haven demand
factors.summary: DXY holding ~100.00 despite Middle East tensions (FXStreet 01:17); USD/JPY reached 6-week highs ~160.55 but intervention fears persist. Mixed signal: safe-haven flows offset by inflation/rate expectations. Multinational Dow exposure (~50% international revenue) faces headwind if USD weakens further.

factors.name: Fed rate expectations rising
factors.summary: Hot US inflation and talk of 'higher-for-longer' rates (FXStreet 23:53) increase discount-rate pressure on dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JNJ, PG). 10-year yields likely to rise, pressuring valuation.

factors.name: Tech earnings pressure (Oracle, AI competition)
factors.summary: Oracle beat earnings but stock dropped on negative free cash flow and $20bn capital raise for data centers (MarketWatch 00:15). OpenAI mulling major price cuts (WSJ 01:47), signaling AI margin compression. Indirect weight on Dow-exposed tech (MSFT, AAPL not in Dow but affects sentiment).

factors.name: China slowdown signals
factors.summary: Alibaba, JD.com slid after Beijing reprimanded e-commerce players (Bloomberg 04:50). PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8150 vs estimate 6.7819, weakening yuan signals demand concerns. Negative for Dow cyclicals dependent on China (CAT, BA, DE, GE).

factors.name: Recent price action: 24h -1.42%, 4h -0.58%
factors.summary: Dow already down modestly but move not yet extended. Suggests market repricing geopolitical risk and inflation in real-time; phase 2 of selloff likely continuing into European/US open.

screening.narrative: The Dow is facing a confluence of headwinds: escalating US-Iran military conflict (second day of strikes, Hormuz closure threats), oil spiking to ~$91, inflation at 3-year highs forcing ECB and Fed to signal higher-for-longer rates, and China growth signals deteriorating. Industrials, financials, and energy-exposed Dow names face margin compression from input-cost inflation and demand slowdown fears. The 24h decline of -1.42% reflects early repricing but move is not yet extended; likely entering phase 2 of a broader selloff into key US/European opens.
catalysts_ahead: ECB policy decision & press conference (12:15 GMT, later today 2026-06-11)
US PPI & jobless claims (Thursday US morning, 2026-06-11)
FOMC meeting outcome (expected mid-June)
China industrial production & retail sales (June calendar)
Dow component earnings (MSFT, GS, UNH, HD, CAT guidance critical)
Straits of Hormuz shipping flow updates & Iran military developments (intraday)
Oil inventory drawdown reports (weekly, can shift WTI >$2)
screening.risk_warning: If US and Iran achieve ceasefire agreement (Trump has claimed talks ongoing), oil crashes back to $80–85, real yields fall, and Dow recovers sharply to 50400+. Additionally, if CPI data next week shows inflation moderating faster than feared, Fed dovish pivot could flip sentiment bullish and invalidate the SELL thesis.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in phase 2; avoid chasing shorts into new lows. Better entry on retest of 49800–49900 resistance or if US opens gap-down and bounces intraday—short 50100–50200 with tight stops. Await ECB decision (12:15 GMT) for volatility; if ECB hikes as expected, confirms hawkish backdrop; if dovish surprise, reassess.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Escalation (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Iran-US military exchange escalated with Strait of Hormuz closure threat, multiple strikes, and collateral damage (Bahrain, Kuwait airspace closure). Markets pricing risk-off despite diplomatic talks 'still on track' per CNN — uncertainty premium is elevated and risk appetite compressed.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility & Supply Disruption Risk
factors.summary: WTI trading ~$87.50, Brent toward $95/bbl on Hormuz closure risk. Energy input costs for Dow industrials (transportation, manufacturing) rising; conflicting signals as some reports note oil declining despite escalation, but fundamental risk remains high.

factors.name: Emerging Market & Risk Asset Selloff
factors.summary: EM stocks down 6th time in 7 days; AUD depressed near 2-month lows; INR selling pressure on oil bounce. Dow has significant international revenue exposure (~50%) — weakness in EM demand signals headwind for multinational corporates.

factors.name: Tech/AI Sector Pressure & Margin Concerns
factors.summary: Oracle facing margin pressure despite cloud growth (per Mizuho/Stifel); ChatGPT price-war/tokenmaxxing complaints; memory chip 'insane prices' stoking hardware cost inflation. Several Dow heavyweights (MSFT exposure via holdings) see AI enthusiasm cooling and pricing pressure emerging.

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Rebound
factors.summary: USD clawing back losses and bouncing in European session amid geopolitical uncertainty; market pricing 24 bps tightening by year-end (unchanged post-CPI). Strong dollar is headwind for Dow's multinational earnings translation.

factors.name: CPI Print In-Line, No Major Surprise
factors.summary: US CPI yesterday came 'mostly in line with expectations' per multiple sources, alleviating hawkish fears but not providing dovish catalyst. No surprise = no directional acceleration; FOMC next week remains the main event.

factors.name: Fed Policy Hold & Rate Trajectory Uncertainty
factors.summary: Fed on hold, market pricing modest tightening by year-end but BoC and ECB in focus today. ECB widely expected to hike 25 bps (already priced); forward guidance and growth downgrades likely — no major US-specific catalyst until FOMC next week.

factors.name: European Central Bank Hike & Growth Downgrade Risk
factors.summary: ECB decision at 12:15 GMT today; expected 25 bps hike + likely growth projections downgrades. EUR/USD under pressure near 2-month lows; tighter European monetary conditions could dampen global growth and Dow multinational demand.

factors.name: China E-Commerce Regulatory Selloff & Growth Concerns
factors.summary: Chinese e-commerce stocks hit by regulatory censure, triggering EM selloff. Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand tied to China growth — regulatory uncertainty and weakness there is a medium-term headwind for Dow industrials.

factors.name: 24h Price Action: -1.42% Decline
factors.summary: Dow already down 1.42% in 24h, suggesting move is in progress (PHASE_2). Yesterday's 'ugly showing' per market reports and tech selloff late session — momentum is directionally bearish but move is not yet extended (requires >2% 24h for PHASE_3).

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior reads 2026-06-11 show SELL 7/10 (Haiku & Sonnet at 00:14) and SELL 6/10 (Haiku at 05:27, no confirmation). This read maintains downside bias but confidence has moderated as initial sell pressure continues without explosive follow-through.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Mega-Cap Tech Enthusiasm
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO and AI mega-listings generating headlines and some futures recovery (S&P +0.8%, Nasdaq +1.3% per morning reports), but Dow is heavier, more cyclical, and exposed to industrials/financials — IPO euphoria has limited direct impact on blue-chip dividends.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between a geopolitical risk-off dynamic (US-Iran escalation, supply chain uncertainty) and a modest tech recovery bid (SpaceX IPO, EM stabilization hopes). The 1.42% 24h decline reflects risk-off sentiment, with emerging markets, commodities, and cyclicals under pressure. USD strength and ECB tightening today will likely keep risk appetite compressed. Although FOMC is next week (a major wildcard), today's ECB decision and ongoing Iran/Strait of Hormuz closure threat are the nearest catalysts — neither appears likely to trigger a sharp Dow rally.
catalysts_ahead: ECB policy decision & forward guidance (12:15 GMT today) — likely 25 bps hike + growth downgrade; EUR/USD reaction will ripple to USD strength and EM demand
US PPI data (timing unclear from feed, but mentioned as next impetus for gold/inflation narrative) — check for surprises in input costs, especially energy-related
FOMC meeting (next week, ~June 18) — dominant long-dated driver; market still pricing modest tightening by year-end
Iran-US diplomatic/military developments (real-time risk) — Hormuz closure or ceasefire collapse could spike oil >$100, disrupt supplies, and trigger secondary flight-to-quality
Dow component earnings & guidance (BA, CAT, HD, UNH, V, JPM ongoing this week/next) — cyclical revisions and margin commentary critical for index repricing
China economic data & regulatory moves — watching for PMI, stimulus signals, or further tech sector pressure
Oil (WTI/Brent) continuation — if Hormuz effectively closed, >$95 Brent could inflate input costs and reduce real earnings for industrials
screening.risk_warning: Rapid de-escalation or diplomatic breakthrough in Iran-US talks could trigger sharp risk-on reversal and Dow rally to 50200+. Conversely, if Hormuz truly closes and oil spikes >$100, energy costs could accelerate inflation, forcing Fed hawkishness and rate hikes sooner than priced — that would extend the sell-off. FOMC guidance next week is the ultimate bias-flip mechanism; if Fed signals patience/cuts, entire short thesis collapses. Watch for ECB forward guidance softening (dovish surprise) as an upside tail risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in mid-phase (PHASE_2); consider short entries on intraday retests to 49950–50050 (resistance of past 4h candles). Avoid chasing the decline below 49700 until geopolitical clarity or a pullback retest emerges. If Dow reclaims 50200 on ECB disappointment or positive Iran news, exit shorts and reassess.

factors.name: US PPI Inflation Surprise
factors.summary: PPI +6.5% y/y (May) vs +6.4% expected, with headline +1.1% m/m vs +0.7% expected—fastest pace since Nov 2022 per Bloomberg. Iran war energy shock driving pipeline inflation; pressures Dow industrials' input costs.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike & Hawkish Pivot
factors.summary: ECB raised rates 25bp to 2.40% (first hike since 2023) and lifted inflation forecasts to 3.0% for 2026. Lagarde warned inflation spreading beyond energy. Signals divergence from Fed dovishness, potentially supporting USD and pressuring risk sentiment.

factors.name: US Initial Jobless Claims
factors.summary: Initial claims 229K vs 219K estimate, continuing claims 1.795M vs 1.780M estimate. Slightly hotter than expected but still within normal range; no major deterioration signal for Dow employment-sensitive names.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk: Iran-US Escalation
factors.summary: Trump threatens strikes 'VERY HARD TONIGHT' and plans to seize Kharg Island oil infrastructure. Supply disruption fears, though oil temporarily eased as Hormuz flows up 50% m/m and Chinese demand fell. Elevated uncertainty headwind for multinationals; Dow has ~50% international exposure.

factors.name: World Bank Growth Cuts
factors.summary: Global GDP growth cut to 2.5% from 2.6%; Eurozone slashed to 0.8% from 0.9%; Middle East cut to 1.6% from 4.3%. Two-thirds of economies facing deteriorating prospects. Cyclical headwind for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, DE) dependent on global capex.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD/CAD at fresh 2026 highs ~1.3990 on safe-haven demand and ECB hiking. Strong USD pressures Dow's ~50% international revenue; translation headwind for earnings.

factors.name: Chip Sector Rally
factors.summary: Semiconductors a bright spot Thursday (Bloomberg reports); Samsung +150% YTD despite insider probe. Dow exposure via Intel and semiconductor-adjacent names provides modest tailwind, though concentrated in narrow cohort.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Hype & FOMO
factors.summary: SpaceX drew >$70B in retail pre-orders; analysts warn of 'wild-eyed FOMO' and concentration risk. Retail euphoria may support risk appetite short-term, but also signals late-cycle froth and potential for volatility; no direct Dow impact.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-End Outlook
factors.summary: Gold fell to YTD low on higher-for-longer rate expectations (ECB, hawkish inflation data). 10Y yield likely supported; rate-sensitive dividend payers (utilities, REITs) in Dow face headwind. Macro hawkishness spreading.

factors.name: AI Concentration Warning
factors.summary: Nomura strategist warns Big Tech concentration preventing new highs; hedge funds split on riding AI vs. positioning for correction. Underscores fragility of market breadth; Dow's industrial/cyclical tilt offers relative defensiveness but lacks AI upside.

key_levels.resistance: ~39,500 (intraday high, bear-flag zone per Dow analysis)

screening.narrative: US30 is caught in a two-way tug: the PPI beat, ECB hike, and World Bank growth cuts are classic bearish signals for cyclical industrials, while geopolitical premium and safe-haven demand support USD and reduce Dow's international revenue outlook. Chip sector strength and SpaceX IPO FOMO offer tactical support, but underlying macro tone has shifted hawkish (central banks, inflation surprise, growth downgrades). The move higher intraday is partial recovery after two days of losses, placing us in Phase 2—not yet extended, but late-cycle exuberance vs. deteriorating fundamentals creates conflict.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX IPO debut Friday (retail allocation cap, lock-up constraints may drive volatility)
Trump Iran strikes 'tonight' (further geopolitical uncertainty, oil supply shock)
Next FOMC decision (rate hold expected, but forward guidance critical given ECB pivot and PPI surprise)
Dow heavyweight earnings season (UNH, MSFT, GS earnings calls for forward guidance on capex, margins)
China PMI/activity data (export demand proxy for Dow industrials CAT, BA, DE)
Weekly jobless claims (labor market resilience check; next release in 7 days)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would flip BULLISH if: (1) Trump-Iran negotiations produce sudden de-escalation, easing energy/inflation fears; (2) Fed signals imminent dovish pivot despite PPI, supporting equities over rates; (3) Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA, DE) report better-than-feared guidance and stabilize industrial demand outlook. Conversely, bias would turn MORE BEARISH if geopolitical tensions spike (Kharg Island seizure attempt), oil surges >$100/bbl, or credit spreads widen—signaling financial stress.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow is testing bear-flag decision zone; move intraday is partial recovery, not extended. Wait for close at or above ~39,500 resistance + confirmation that chip/tech strength is broad, NOT just FOMO. Otherwise, consider short entry on retest of ~39,300 if geopolitical risk re-escalates or earnings disappoint. Do NOT chase higher into SpaceX IPO hype without Dow-specific earnings confirmation.

factors.name: Iran Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: Trump cancelled scheduled strikes on Iran and announced a diplomatic deal with confirmation from Israel's N12, eliminating tail risk. Markets surged immediately: S&P 500 +1.3%, Russell 2000 +2.4%, removing a major 'shock event' overhang.

factors.name: Oil Price Relief
factors.summary: WTI retreated to ~$89.50 (down 0.86% on day) as US-Iran diplomacy tempered supply fears. Lower energy input costs are positive for Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON) and transportation logistics.

factors.name: Hot PPI Data (May +1.1% vs +0.7% expected)
factors.summary: Producer prices rose faster than consensus, driven by energy war fallout. This suggests persistent input-cost inflation and may support Fed hawkishness, pressuring rate-sensitive Dow dividend names and financials.

factors.name: Mortgage Rates at Year-High (6.52%)
factors.summary: 30-year fixed mortgage jumped from 6.48% to 6.52%—highest of the year—pressuring housing-sensitive Dow constituents (HD) and real-estate-dependent segments.

factors.name: Treasury Bond Auction Weakness (30-year tail)
factors.summary: US auctioned $22B of 30-year bonds at 5.020% vs 5.008% WI with a 1.2 bps tail and 2.30x bid-to-cover, a weak sign amid Congress debating further spending. Rising long-end yields challenge dividend valuations.

factors.name: Russell 2000 Outperformance (+2.4%)
factors.summary: Small-cap rally after Iran deal and SpaceX IPO enthusiasm reflects broad risk-on sentiment. Dow's cyclical/industrial tilt aligns with rotation out of mega-cap tech into value/cyclicals.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Hype & Capital Flow
factors.summary: BlackRock targeting ~$5B allocation; $70B retail orders reported. Massive liquidity event could generate spillover bid for risk assets, but execution risks and extreme volatility expected (analyst warning).

factors.name: Oracle Earnings Miss / Cash Concerns
factors.summary: Oracle tumbled 11% on negative free cash flow and $95B capex plan despite beat, raising questions about AI capex ROI. Suggests mega-cap tech earnings quality deteriorating, potential headwind for Dow-adjacent cloud/software names.

factors.name: ECB Rate Hike (June) + July Pause Signal
factors.summary: ECB delivered 25bp hike Thursday; sources indicate July pause likely if energy prices hold. Reflects divergent policy path vs Fed; supports USD strength but signals global growth caution.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Post-PPI, Post-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: USD rally driven by hotter inflation data and geopolitical relief bid. Stronger dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue, but inflation signal supports commodity and energy exposure.

factors.name: Gold Breakdown to 6-Month Low
factors.summary: XAU/USD fell to $4,023 (6-month low), trimmed to $4,091 by session close. Suggests real-yield repricing higher and deflates inflation-hedge demand; risk-off signal mixed with rate-hike story.

factors.name: VIX / Credit Spread Implications
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in feed, but Iran de-escalation + SpaceX liquidity event create conflicting cross-currents. Geopolitical tail risk off, but bond-auction weakness + rising rates hint at credit sensitivity.

screening.narrative: US30 is staging a relief rally following Trump's cancellation of Iran strikes and announcement of a diplomatic deal, eliminating acute geopolitical shock risk and sending oil lower (~$89.50). However, this rally is partially offset by a **hotter-than-expected PPI print** (+1.1% vs +0.7% expected), which reignites inflation concerns and supports Fed hawkishness, keeping long-end yields elevated (30-year mortgage at 6.52%, Treasury auctions showing weakness). The Russell 2000's outperformance (+2.4%) vs mega-cap tech (Oracle tumble -11%) reflects a tactical 'risk-on, value rotation' story, which is structurally positive for Dow industrials and cyclicals but challenged by rising real yields. **Price action shows +0.76% in 4h and +0.79% in 24h—a clean phase-2 rally underway, not extended yet.**
catalysts_ahead: Fed decision (timing TBD, but data-dependent on CPI/PCE next week)
SpaceX IPO pricing/opening tomorrow (massive liquidity event, volatility catalyst)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (next release: likely mid-next week, critical for Dow's industrial content)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (trailing indicators, but sentiment drivers)
China PMI & trade data (CAT/BA demand signals)
Bank of Japan June rate decision (25bp hike likely per ING; affects USD/JPY and carry flows)
Oil price action (if Brent moves above $100, inflation repricing accelerates)
Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD guidance on rate sensitivity, capex plans)
screening.risk_warning: **Inflation repricing risk:** If PPI remains elevated and Fed signals hawkish hold or continued hikes, the bond-market weakness will intensify, pressuring dividend-heavy Dow names and financials. A spike in 10-year yields above 4.5% could reverse the rally quickly. **Geopolitical reversal:** Any escalation in Iran/Middle East conflict after today's deal announcement would collapse this relief and send oil/gold spiking, inverting the current bias. **SpaceX execution/crowding:** If the IPO disappoints post-hype or sucks massive liquidity out of the broader market, Dow could see a selloff. **Earnings quality:** Oracle-like warnings about AI capex ROI and negative free cash flow suggest mega-cap earnings growth story is cracking; this rotational pressure could flip sentiment back to caution.
screening.entry_guidance: Rally is in phase 2 after Iran deal relief—wait for a retest of 50,400–50,500 support before adding longs; or chase break above 50,800 resistance only if PPI fears fade and 10-year yields stabilize below 4.35%. Avoid chasing at extremes into SpaceX IPO event tomorrow given event volatility.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway on Iran deal headline; consider entries on any pullback to 50,400-50,450 range or wait for tomorrow's open confirmation.

factors.name: US-Iran De-escalation
factors.summary: Trump cancelled scheduled strikes on Iran, announcing an agreement approved by all parties (Investinglive 17:29, 17:51). Markets surged immediately with S&P +1.3%, Nasdaq +1.8%, Russell 2000 +2.4%. Major geopolitical risk-off catalyst removed, highly bullish for cyclicals and industrials in Dow.

factors.name: Oil Price Retreat
factors.summary: WTI retreated to $89.50 (-0.86%) on Iran diplomacy tempering supply fears (FXStreet 16:26). Lower energy input costs benefit Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON) and reduce inflation pressures on margins. CVX direct negative but offset by broader industrial benefit.

factors.name: PPI Inflation Report
factors.summary: US producer prices rose 1.1% in May vs 0.7% expected, fastest pace in 3+ years driven by Iran war fallout (CNBC 16:25, Bloomberg 17:56). Hot input cost inflation pressures margins for Dow industrials and suggests Fed may stay higher for longer.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Hot PPI reinforces higher-for-longer narrative. BNP Paribas notes US breakevens should be higher (Bloomberg 17:56). ECB hiked and IMF says needs to keep hiking (Bloomberg 15:45). Rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend payers face headwind from extended high-rate environment.

factors.name: Risk-On Rotation
factors.summary: Russell 2000 +2.4% surge indicates strong risk appetite and rotation into cyclicals, which favors Dow's industrial-heavy composition. Small-cap outperformance typically signals confidence in domestic economic activity.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Collapse
factors.summary: Gold fell to 6-month low at $4,023 (FXStreet 17:30, US Top News 15:51) despite inflation fears, indicating geopolitical risk premium evaporating. Safe-haven outflows support risk assets. VIX likely compressed on deal news.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Overhang
factors.summary: Massive $1.8T SpaceX IPO launching with $70B retail orders (Investinglive 15:07). S&P 500 gave back gains and turned negative (Investinglive 15:07) amid liquidity concerns. Supply/demand imbalance could pressure broader indices including Dow as capital gets reallocated.

factors.name: Treasury Yields
factors.summary: 30-year bond auction tailed 1.2 bps at 5.020% vs 5.008% WI (Investinglive 17:03), described as 'bad sign' with Congress debating more spending. Rising long-end yields pressure Dow dividend payers and rate-sensitive names.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD strengthening on hot PPI and Fed repricing (FXStreet 15:34, 16:02). Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure; stronger dollar creates translation headwinds for multinationals like CAT, BA, MSFT, AAPL within the index.

factors.name: Technical Chart Signal
factors.summary: MarketWatch (18:03) reports 'S&P 500 chart breaking down but new buy signal flashing green' with bullish NYSE activity and hidden shift in volatility. Suggests institutional positioning for upside despite surface weakness.

confirmation.narrative: Dow rallied +0.76% (4h) on sudden US-Iran peace deal, removing major geopolitical tail risk and collapsing the oil/war premium that had pressured cyclicals. Markets surged immediately with small-caps leading (+2.4% Russell), signaling strong risk appetite favoring Dow's industrial composition. However, hot PPI (+1.1% vs 0.7% exp) reinforces Fed higher-for-longer, and SpaceX IPO liquidity drain caused S&P to reverse gains. Net bullish on geopolitical relief but monitoring rate/liquidity headwinds.

SpaceX IPO pricing and launch (imminent, major liquidity event)
CPI report (upcoming, critical after hot PPI)
China PMI/economic data (affects CAT, BA, materials demand)
Dow component earnings (any guidance updates)

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Risk Removal

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD Index retreated sharply (largest drop in >1 month per FXStreet) as geopolitical de-escalation reduced safe-haven demand. Weaker dollar supports US multinational earnings translation for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: Crude fell to 8-week lows after Trump Iran announcement. Lower energy input costs benefit Dow industrials (CAT, HON, GE, MMM); reduces inflation pressure on consumer discretionary; supports airline/transport margins.

factors.name: Risk Appetite / SPaceX IPO Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO priced at $135/share for $75B raise (largest IPO ever, $1.77T market cap), sparking broad risk-on flows. Bank stocks hit record highs Thursday on IPO optimism and Iran deal hopes. Retail FOMO and leveraged ETF launches suggest sustained appetite.

factors.name: Treasury Yields (Long-End Pressure)
factors.summary: 30-year bond auction tailed at 5.020% vs 5.008% WI, with weak bid-to-cover (2.30x prior). Signals potential yield stickiness and rising term premium despite geopolitical relief, which could pressure dividend-heavy Dow names over time.

factors.name: Sector Rotation into Cyclicals
factors.summary: Russell 2000 +2.4% on Iran news (Thursday close), outpacing S&P 500 +1.3%, indicating rotation from mega-cap tech into small/mid-cap industrials and cyclicals—direct tailwind for Dow composition (CAT, MMM, BA, GE, HON all benefit).

screening.narrative: US30 surged +1.86% Thursday on Trump's Iran de-escalation announcement and SpaceX mega-IPO momentum, alongside broad risk-on driven by dollar weakness and oil price decline. The move is fresh (PHASE_2) but already extended by 1.25% in 24h and 0.52% in 4h, indicating buying has been brisk. Dow's composition—heavy in cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE, HON, MMM) and financials (JPM, GS)—benefits from geopolitical relief, weaker USD, lower oil, and sector rotation away from mega-cap tech. However, longer-dated Treasury yields remain sticky (30-year auction weak), and core fundamentals (ISM, earnings guidance, China growth) remain unconfirmed. This is a relief rally with tactical tailwinds, not yet a structural reversal.

SpaceX mega-IPO optimism and bank stocks at record highs add fuel. factors.name: US-Iran De-escalation & Geopolitical Risk Premium Removal
factors.summary: Trump cancelled planned strikes and announced framework deal near completion, expected signing within days. This removes major geopolitical risk premium from commodities and equities, particularly benefiting bank stocks and multinational industrials with supply-chain exposure.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse (WTI to ~$85, 2-month low)
factors.summary: WTI dropped sharply on Trump's de-escalation narrative, falling to lowest since mid-April. Lower energy input costs are net-positive for Dow industrials and transportation/logistics components; reduces CPI-inflation concerns temporarily.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness (DXY fell most in >1 month)
factors.summary: USD retreated sharply from 3-month highs on de-escalation and risk-off reversal. Weaker dollar benefits Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure and multinationals (BA, CAT, PG, MSFT translation gains).

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Historic Demand & Market Sentiment Lift
factors.summary: SpaceX raised record $75B IPO at $135/share, valuations suggest $1.77T–$2T+ market cap, generating massive venture/institutional investor euphoria. Equity breadth and risk appetite sentiment now elevated; IPO mania supports broader equity beta.

factors.name: Bank Stock Record Highs (Rates/Deal Optimism)
factors.summary: Financials hit all-time highs on deal relief and rate-sensitive bond rally (10-yr yields declining on risk-off). JPM, GS, BAC strength supports Dow's financial weight and broader cyclical rotation.

factors.name: Iran Deal Lacks Formal Tehran Approval (Execution Risk)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Iran NOT on Trump's list of countries that have approved the deal; Foreign Ministry disputes scope; tanker blockade reported at Hormuz. 'Schrödinger's cat' deal — signed and unsigned simultaneously — introduces tail risk of reversal if talks collapse.

factors.name: Insider Selling (Cisco EVP, FormFactor CEO, Expensify C-suite)
factors.summary: Multiple Form 4 filings show executives dumping shares ($1.44M FormFactor, $887K+ Cisco transactions, $10K+ Expensify CEO) — classic euphoria-phase distribution. Insiders rotating to cash ahead of potential pullback.

factors.name: Adobe & Oracle Software Weakness (11% Oracle tumble, Adobe 7-year lows)
factors.summary: Oracle beat earnings but tanked 11% on $95B capex plan and negative FCF concerns; Adobe losing CFO, trading at 7-year lows. Tech leadership fracturing despite strong macro data. Signals sector rotation away from mega-cap cloud/AI, relevant to Dow's tech exposure (MSFT, AAPL).

factors.name: Housing Data Softness (Lennar Q3 deliveries below estimates)
factors.summary: Homebuilder Lennar warns Q3 deliveries below guidance — indicator of consumer spending slowdown, mortgage stress at 6.52% rates. Dow exposure to HD, JPM mortgage origination under pressure.

factors.name: New Zealand Manufacturing PMI Falls Below 50 (49.9, May)
factors.summary: NZ BNZ-BusinessNZ PMI dipped below contraction threshold; energy/demand headwinds cited. Leading indicator for global industrial softness; concerns for Dow exporters (CAT, BA, GE machinery demand).

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Stable; No Major Catalyst This Week
factors.summary: No FOMC scheduled imminent; ECB reportedly seeing July pause if energy prices hold. Rate-sensitive backdrop supportive, but no dovish surprise to anchor lower yields; real yields likely stabilizing.

SpaceX's record $75B IPO euphoria lifted risk appetite and bank stocks to all-time highs. However, the Iran deal lacks formal Iranian approval, insiders are dumping shares into the rally, software leaders (Oracle, Adobe) are cratering on profitability/capex fears, and housing data is weakening. The move is already +1.25% in 24h (PHASE_2), so momentum has run; retest likely before fresh leg higher.

screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or Tehran refuses to sign, risk-off reversal would send oil spiking, dollar rallying, and equities lower—erasing geopolitical premium gains. Software/cloud selloff (Oracle, Adobe) may spread to Dow's MSFT, AAPL if AI capex doubts intensify. Housing weakness (Lennar warning) could cascade into consumer-facing Dow names (HD, JNJ) if mortgage rates stay elevated. Insider selling into euphoria is a classic late-cycle topping signal; any disappointment Friday (ISM data, jobless claims miss) could trigger sharp mean reversion.
